pr.title: Local Runner: fix Windows-safe invocation via direct venv module execution (no recursive poetry) (#52 rework)
pr.description:
## Summary

Reworks PR #52 (`_windows_safe_command`), which was **broken on Windows**. #52 rewrote a leading `poetry ...` to `[sys.executable, "-m", "poetry", ...]`, but the Local Runner runs *inside* Poetry's managed project venv, whose interpreter has **no `poetry` module** — so the rewrite self-defeated with `No module named poetry`. The bare `.exe` shim path had already failed with WDAC `WinError 4551`. Net: Local Runner tool actions never ran on Windows.

Fix: on Windows, drop the poetry wrapper entirely and invoke project-installed tools **directly with the current venv interpreter**:

```
poetry run ruff ...    ->  [sys.executable, "-m", "ruff",     ...]
poetry run pytest ...  ->  [sys.executable, "-m", "pytest",   ...]
poetry run axiom ...   ->  [sys.executable, "-m", "axiom_cli", ...]
git/dotnet/powershell  ->  unchanged
```

Driven by a small table `_WINDOWS_TOOL_MODULE = {"ruff": "ruff", "pytest": "pytest", "axiom": "axiom_cli"}`. Only a leading `poetry run <tool> ...` shape is rewritten; anything else is untouched. **No-op off Windows** (`is_windows=False`), so Linux/CI keep `poetry run ...`.

`axiom` needs a module target because `axiom.exe` is WDAC-blocked and `axiom_core.cmd` does not exist. New file `src/axiom_cli/__main__.py` provides one, calling the **same callable** as the console script (`[tool.poetry.scripts] axiom = "axiom_cli.main:cli"`):

```python
from axiom_cli.main import cli
if __name__ == "__main__":
    cli()
```

so `python -m axiom_cli ...` is an exact equivalent of `axiom ...` on both platforms.

## What changed
- `tools/local_runner/local_runner.py` — rewrote `_windows_safe_command`; replaced `_WINDOWS_MODULE_TOOLS` with `_WINDOWS_TOOL_MODULE`.
- `src/axiom_cli/__main__.py` (new) — module entrypoint backed by `axiom_cli.main:cli`.
- `tests/test_local_runner.py` — rewrote `TestWindowsSafeCommand`; added `TestAxiomCliModuleEntrypoint` (`python -m axiom_cli --help` / `execution-chain-run --help` exit 0; `__main__.cli is main.cli`).
- Docs/logs: `docs/runbooks/local-runner-runbook.md` (Windows-safe section), `docs/logs/bug-validation-log.md` (BUG-020), `docs/logs/behavior-change-ledger.md` (BHV-033).

## What did NOT change
- The allowlist remains the command boundary; no new action names, task schema, or workspace policy.
- No arbitrary-argument execution — pure argv transform of an already-allowlisted command.
- No promotion / confidence / readiness / evidence semantics; no Revit behavior; 2024 baseline unaffected.
- Poetry is **not** installed into the project venv.

## Tests run
- `ruff check` on changed files — clean.
- `pytest tests/test_local_runner.py` — 66 passed, 1 skipped.
- `python -m axiom_cli --help` and `execution-chain-run --help` — exit 0.
- Broader targeted set (`test_command_registry`, `test_execution_chain_orchestrator`, `test_evidence_promotion`): all pass except the **pre-existing** `test_all_builtin_axiom_commands_cataloged` drift (`context-preflight`, `model-health-evidence-apply`, `model-health-evidence-history`) — confirmed failing on `main` independently of this change, unrelated to the runner.

## Validation still pending
Windows lane-2 is **not** claimed validated. Requires operator re-run on `C:\Dev\Axiom\Code\Axiom-platform` after merge: Local Runner `ruff` / `execution_chain_run` / `capability_evidence_apply` should no longer hit `WinError 4551` or `No module named poetry`.

## Factory relevance
Infrastructure/runner hardening that unblocks the Windows lane-2 evidence path (execution-chain + evidence-apply loop) so the verification factory can run end-to-end on the target environment.


Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7
Requested by: @plamen-dev
pr.author: plamen-dev

commits.author: plamen.hristov@imsdevlab.onmicrosoft.com
commits.message:
Local Runner: fix Windows-safe invocation via direct venv module execution (no recursive poetry) (#52 rework)

Drop the poetry wrapper on Windows: rewrite poetry run <tool> ... to
[sys.executable, -m, <module>, ...]. Add src/axiom_cli/__main__.py module
entrypoint backed by axiom_cli.main:cli so axiom normalizes to
python -m axiom_cli. Fixes BUG-020 (No module named poetry); BHV-033.

Co-Authored-By: Devin AI <158243242+devin-ai-integration[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
